headline: Jordan Bardella, a princess and 'Paris Match': The story of a PR gamble
description: The relationship between Marine Le Pen's lieutenant and an aristocratic student raised in the international jet set made the front page of 'Paris Match.' The media empire of Vincent Bolloré has seized the opportunity to 'glamorize' the likely far-right presidential candidate.

headline: Trump vows US will 'eliminate' any Iranian ships that approach blockade
With Orban's defeat, the French far right loses a key ally
Lafarge ex-CEO sentenced to six years in prison for funding jihadists in Syria
description: The French company, since acquired by the Swiss group Holcim, and former executives were charged with paying several million euros to armed jihadist groups in 2013–2014.

headline: Parenting: Why the morning rush drives us crazy
description: Le Monde's parenting columnist, Clara Georges, breaks down one of her typically hectic family mornings, drawing on the insights of philosopher Pierre Vesperini and the solutions proposed by feminist author Titiou Lecoq.

headline: Quebec's maple syrup harvest weakened by climate change
description: As the world's leading producer of maple syrup, Quebec is working to better adapt its industry to increasingly unpredictable climate conditions.

headline: 'Lebanon's resilience is celebrated, as if survival were admirable rather than imposed'
description: Young Lebanese journalist Tayma Saliba writes about being from a country shaped by conflict, where war has infiltrated conversations for generations, in an op-ed for Le Monde.

Pope embarks on African tour, a strategic continent for Catholicism
description: From April 13 to 23, Pope Léon XIV is set to visit Algeria, Cameroon, Angola and Equatorial Guinea. The trip symbolizes the shift in the center of gravity of the Catholic Church, whose future now lies in Africa.

headline: Hungary celebrates 'return to Europe' after Orban's crushing electoral defeat
description: Péter Magyar's party, Tisza, won more than 53% of the vote in the legislative elections, far ahead of the prime minister's Fidesz, which managed barely 38%. Tisza is expected to secure 138 out of the 199 seats in Parliament, giving it a constitutional supermajority.

headline: The planning battle to revive Tour Montparnasse, Paris's only skyscraper
description: After more than 10 years of negotiations between Paris City Hall and the tower's co-owners, the transformation of the now-empty Tour Montparnasse and its property complex is set to begin in autumn 2026.
